cards.title: PagedWeight: Dynamic Runtime Quantization Balances MoE Weights Against KV Cache
cards.why_it_matters: If you serve MoE models at scale, memory contention between expert weights and KV cache is a real bottleneck — this directly addresses that tradeoff with concrete throughput numbers.
cards.summary: PagedWeight dynamically quantizes MoE expert weights at runtime to free GPU memory for KV cache. It achieves FP16-equivalent accuracy with up to 72% GPU memory savings and 1.94× throughput improvement, and improves quality over static quantization baselines by up to 39.3% at similar memory budgets with at most 4.1% throughput loss.

cards.title: Thermodynamic Computing Blueprint Uses Superconducting Analog Circuits for Energy-Efficient ML
cards.why_it_matters: As a frontier AI/hardware watcher, this outlines a physically grounded alternative compute paradigm — Langevin-dynamics-based analog hardware — with preliminary superconducting circuit experiments rather than pure theory.
cards.summary: The paper proposes a thermodynamic computing stack using stochastic analog hardware governed by Langevin dynamics with tunable energy potentials, framing popular ML models as probabilistic graphical models trainable on this substrate. A preliminary experimental realization uses stochastic analog superconducting circuits driven by thermal noise, with runtime and energy analysis included.

cards.title: PEARL Fuses Adjoint-Based Optimal Control with RL for High-Dimensional Dynamical Systems
cards.why_it_matters: Scaling RL to high-dimensional continuous control without low-dimensional state reductions is a core research challenge — PEARL's actor-adjoint algorithm offers a physics-grounded path forward.
cards.summary: PEARL (Physics-EnhAnced Reinforcement Learning) combines an actor network with adjoint-based policy gradient computation over short horizons and neural-network-approximated future-return sensitivities, exploiting differentiable physics to cut environment interactions. Tested on parametric navigation in unsteady flows, it outperforms standard RL baselines, generalizes across parameter regimes, and scales to high-dimensional state-action spaces.

cards.title: Laplacian Optimal Transport Enables Cluster-Aware Point Cloud Matching
cards.why_it_matters: Optimal transport with structure-preserving regularization is directly relevant to researchers working on distribution alignment, domain adaptation, or geometric deep learning.
cards.summary: LapOT regularizes the optimal transport problem with quadratic Laplacian terms built from similarity graphs, encouraging couplings that respect cluster structure in both point sets. A companion method, Refined Simultaneous Clustering (RSC), uses the LapOT coupling to produce consistent cross-set partitions, overcoming instabilities from independent clustering.

cards.title: Open-Weight LLMs Evaluated for Auto-Generating STIX Threat Intelligence from CVE Descriptions
cards.why_it_matters: Benchmarking 11 open-weight models (4B–120B) on structured security output generation is useful context for anyone building agentic or domain-specific NLP pipelines over technical corpora.
cards.summary: The CAV-STIXGen dataset maps autonomous vehicle CVE descriptions to STIX domain objects, relationship objects, CWE, and MITRE ATT&CK entries. Single-model F1 scores reach 0.94 for SDOs and 0.99 for CWE mapping, but only 0.63 for SROs; MITRE ATT&CK mapping remains difficult. A Gemma-4-31B and Codestral-22B multi-agent setup achieves 0.91 SDO F1 and 0.43 SRO F1.
